title: 10 best herbal creams for red eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with red eyes by soothing and calming the affected area.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal aloe creams, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye irritation, promoting overall eye health.

Sothiating and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's unique polysaccharides and glycoproteins provide intense hydration and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or irritants.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel of the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and fatty acids, which work together to reduce swelling and calm irritated eyes. The cooling sensation of aloe vera cream can provide instant relief from redness, itchiness, and dryness, making it an effective remedy for people experiencing eye strain or irritation. Regular use can also promote eye health and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to redness, and also have antimicrobial properties to prevent infections that can exacerbate red eyes.

Vitamins and minerals (especially vitamin E and zinc): These antioxidants help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing redness and inflammation, and also promote healing and regeneration of tissues.
Acemannan: This polysaccharide has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, and also promote healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Aloe vera in creams for red eyes due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help soothe and calm red eyes.
Extract from Leaves: The extract from Aloe vera leaves contains aloin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Combine 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 5 minutes stirring constantly to melt the oil.
Remove the bowl from the heat and add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Whisk the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a small glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate red eye symptoms such as itchiness and dryness.
Soften and lubricate properties: Pot marigold contains mucilages that help soften and lubricate the eyes, reducing discomfort and irritation associated with dry or irritated eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to reduce swelling and irritation, thereby alleviating redness and discomfort. By promoting healing and reducing inflammation, Calendula officinalis creams can provide quick relief from irritated and inflamed eyes, leaving them feeling calm, comfortable, and back to normal. Its natural and gentle approach makes it an effective remedy for soothing red eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help alleviate redness and inflammation in the eyes by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the healing of damaged tissue.
Triterpenoids: These compounds, particularly calendulic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm red, irritated eyes, reducing swelling and promoting comfort.

Naphthoquinones (Plumbagin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections and reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and soothing effects, which can help reduce redness and discomfort in the eyes.
Seeds: They are used due to their ability to enhance the overall potency of the cream and provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom and dry them completely within 2 weeks.
Steep 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of the infused oil to the melted mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which helps reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants.
Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.
Soothing and calming agents: The cream's soothing and calming agents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help calm irritated and inflamed eye tissues, providing relief from redness and itchiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The Echinacea angustifolia plant contains compounds that soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. Its cooling and moisturizing effects provide quick relief from eye strain and discomfort caused by allergies, dry air, or prolonged screen time. Additionally, the cream's natural antimicrobial properties help combat infections that may contribute to red eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with red eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids, such as echinamine, have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat underlying infections that contribute to red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for red eyes because of their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for red eyes due to their potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall when the plant is dormant and dry the roots for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1000mg of the ground echinacea root powder with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the herbal echinacea angustifolia cream in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes, easing redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba cream help protect the delicate eye tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and irritation.
Vasodilatory: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilating properties help dilate blood vessels around the eyes, increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, which can also alleviate redness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with red eyes because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from the Ginkgo biloba leaves has been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By reducing puffiness and irritation, Ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate red eyes caused by fatigue, allergies, or exposure to irritants. Its soothing and moisturizing effects also help to calm and comfort the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which can alleviate red, irritated eyes.
Bilobalide: This triterpene acts as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and inflammation, thus soothing red eyes.
Quercetin: As a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, quercetin helps reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm red, irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate red, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier.
Combine 30-60 grams of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat it gently until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort cream helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in St. John's Wort cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and itching associated with red eyes.

Antiviral properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses that can cause eye infections, such as herpes simplex virus and adenovirus, which may contribute to redness and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The active compounds in Hypericum perforatum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate allergic reactions that can cause redness and irritation in the eyes. By soothing and calming the affected area, these creams can help to reduce redness and discomfort, promoting a more comfortable and clear vision. This natural remedy can provide relief from red eyes caused by allergies or irritation.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These compounds have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and viral replication in the conjunctiva, leading to relief from red eyes.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help mitigate the inflammatory response and oxidative stress in the conjunctiva, thereby reducing redness and discomfort.

Tannins (Gallotannins and Ellagic Acid): These polyphenolic compounds have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and alleviate redness in the conjunctiva by tightening the tissues and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe red eyes.
Flowers: They are a rich source of hyperforin and hypericin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and irritation.
Stems: They contain bioflavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak potency in early summer morning dew.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to preserve their properties.
Infuse 20g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lavender oil, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and eye irritation, promoting healthy eyes and reducing redness.

Calming and soothing properties: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and discomfort that can exacerbate redness and promote a sense of calm and relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural antioxidants present in lavender oil can reduce puffiness and irritation, providing immediate relief from red, itchy eyes. The calming effects of lavender also help to relax the eyes and surrounding muscles, reducing strain and promoting healthy eye function. Regular use of lavender creams can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of red eye episodes.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene ester, it has a sedative effect, which can help alleviate eye irritation and reduce redness caused by stress or fatigue.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, leading to a reduction in redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and soothe the eyes.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and calm the eyes.
Seeds: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent infections around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers when in full bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 40c for 2 hours to preserve the essential oils.
Steep 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500ml of sweet almond oil for 4 hours under the sun.
Mix the infused oil with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 70c.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, providing quick relief from redness and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and calm the eyes. This helps to alleviate redness and irritation caused by allergies, dryness, or exposure to irritants. By applying chamomile cream to the eyes, individuals can experience a sense of relief and comfort, allowing their eyes to return to a normal, healthy state.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin, a flavone compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.

Furanocoumarins (specifically, isoumarin and bergamottin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce redness and prevent infections that may be contributing to red eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: Matricaria chamomilla seeds are used to make creams for red eyes due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Matricaria chamomilla leaves are used to make creams for red eyes due to their soothing and calming effects on the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 1:5 ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Combine the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax and a natural emulsifier like cetyl alcohol.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.

remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with red eyes, providing a soothing effect.
Melissic acid: A phenolic acid, melissic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes.
Linalool: A terpene, linalool has a calming effect on the eyes, reducing redness and promoting relaxation, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and reduce redness in the eyes.
Flowers: They have been traditionally used to treat eye irritations and are a common ingredient in herbal remedies for red eyes due to their calming and anti-inflammatory effects.

Stems: They contain a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them beneficial for soothing red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at a temperature of 40°c for 2 hours.
Infuse 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10 grams of beeswax to the oil.
Heat the oil and beeswax mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 10 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce puffiness, swelling, and redness in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to eye tissues, leading to redness and irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with red eyes because it contains antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort. The cream's soothing and cooling effect also helps to calm the eyes, making it an effective remedy for people who experience red, irritated eyes due to allergies, dryness, or overuse of digital devices. It promotes healthy eye function and provides quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate redness in the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and improving blood vessel function.

Vitamins A and C: These antioxidants help protect the delicate tissues in the eyes from damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote the healing of irritated or inflamed eye areas, thus alleviating redness.

Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants found in Elderberry have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for red eyes because they contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for red eyes because they have antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for red eyes because they contain tannins and other compounds that have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/3 cup of sambucus nigra berries from a wild or cultivated source and dry them for 3 days.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried sambucus nigra berries with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the infused oil and heat mixture gently.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritated or inflamed conjunctivitis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye irritation and redness.
Soothing and calming effects: Dandelion cream's soothing and calming effects help reduce itchiness, burning, and discomfort associated with dry or irritated eyes, promoting relaxation and ease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eyes. The cream's ability to calm and cool the skin around the eyes also helps to reduce redness and irritation, providing instant relief from tired, irritated eyes. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for red, itchy, or irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin found in Dandelion, taraxasterol has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate red, irritated eyes.

Chlorogenic acid: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant properties, chlorogenic acid can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating red eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for red eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for red eyes because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for red eyes because they contain saponins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to calm and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency when they are in full bloom.
Extract the herb's active compounds by infusing 2 tablespoons of the leaves and flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a smooth blend.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies to form a rich and creamy taraxacum officinale cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the eye area. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work to calm redness and soothe dryness, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving conjunctivitis and other eye irritations. Additionally, nettle creams' antioxidant properties help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors, promoting healthy vision and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle contains compounds like gallic acid, quercetin, and kaempferol that reduce inflammation in the eye, thereby alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle, such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lutein, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye redness and irritation.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The compounds in stinging nettle, like histamine and acetylcholine, can help constrict blood vessels in the eyes, reducing blood flow and alleviating redness and discomfort caused by increased blood circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with red eyes.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves from a well-maintained garden or local nursery ensuring at least 100g of leaves.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator on low setting for 6-8 hours or air-drying method taking 7-10 days.
Grind the dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to obtain 30g of nettle extract.
Mix the nettle extract with 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 20ml of distilled water in a double boiler heating until the mixture melts and blends smoothly taking about 10-15 minutes.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars leaving at least 1/4 inch headspace and let it cool to room temperature before storing in a dark cupboard for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with red eyes because they contain soothing herbs that calm inflammation and reduce puffiness. The antioxidants in these creams also work to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to eye irritation and discoloration. Additionally, the cooling properties of eyebright creams help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of redness and promoting a healthy, natural glow. Overall, eyebright creams provide a natural and effective way to alleviate red eyes and leave your peepers feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright creams containing ingredients like arnica, chamomile, or calendula help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyelids and conjunctiva, which can contribute to redness.

Antioxidant property: Herbal ingredients like bilberry, elderberry, or green tea extract have antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can cause eye fatigue and redness.
Vasoconstrictive property: Eyebright creams containing ingredients like witch hazel, peppermint, or eucalyptus may help constrict blood vessels in the eyes, reducing dilation and subsequent redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Buds: Containing volatile oils that reduce inflammation and ease eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground herb with 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat at 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 20% beeswax with 80% coconut oil and gently melt them together in a double boiler at 60°c to 70°c for 10 minutes.
Combine the filtered herbal extract with the melted wax-coconut oil mixture and stir until it cools and thickens, taking approximately 30 minutes to set completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyelids. The cream's cooling sensation calms the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's antihistamine-like qualities help to neutralize histamine release, a common culprit behind itchy, watery, or bloodshot eyes. By applying fennel cream directly to the affected area, you can experience rapid relief from red eyes caused by allergies, dry air, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes by reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye fatigue and irritation, promoting healthy vision and reducing redness.
Soilting and calming properties: Fennel's soothing compounds, such as mucilage and polysaccharides, help calm and cool the eyes, reducing itchiness, dryness, and discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissue.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare essential oil from mature plants yielding 3-5% essential oil content.
Combine 20 grams of sweet almond oil with 10 grams of beeswax and heat mixture until fully melted within 30 minutes.
Add 15 drops of foeniculum vulgare essential oil to the heated mixture and stir well for uniform distribution.
Remove from heat and let cool at room temperature or in a refrigerator until solidified within 60 minutes.
Whip cooled cream with an electric mixer for 5-10 seconds to restore smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The cream's active compounds, such as camphor and menthol, work to reduce puffiness and ease discomfort, while rosemary's antioxidants combat oxidative stress that can contribute to redness. By gently cooling and nourishing the eyes, herbal rosemary creams provide relief from red, itchy, or tired eyes, promoting a more comfortable and refreshed visual experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to redness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and alleviate itchiness associated with red eyes.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause red eyes, such as conjunctivitis, by killing bacteria on the surface of the eye and preventing them from causing infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these compounds can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 10g of beeswax and stirring until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours, or until it reaches a creamy consistency.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers and store them in an airtight container at a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains a compound called thymol, which has been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. When applied topically as a cream, wild marjoram can help calm and comfort irritated eyes, providing relief from redness caused by allergies, fatigue, or dryness. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further damage, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wild marjoram cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and damage to the eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wild marjoram cream, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating redness and itchiness.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Wild marjoram cream's antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat infections that can cause conjunctivitis and other eye infections, reducing redness and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of wild marjoram have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds of wild marjoram soothe dry, irritated eyes and promote healthy ocular tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest origanum vulgare leaves and flowers, ensure freshness by using within a week of harvesting.
Combine 20 grams of dried oregano herb with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours under low heat, then strain and discard solids before transferring to a clean glass container.
Mix 10% glycerin with the herbal oil infusion, adding 20 grams of beeswax and heating until melted and well combined.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before storage in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyelids and conjunctiva. The natural oils in thyme cream work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or exposure to irritants. Additionally, thyme's antiseptic qualities help to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to redness and puffiness. By applying a thyme-based cream to the affected area, you can enjoy rapid relief from red, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme cream's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause redness and inflammation in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, helping to alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like dry eye syndrome and cataracts.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the thyme plant have been traditionally used to treat eye issues due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and dry them according to your preferred method.
Grind 20 grams of dried thyme into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered thyme with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes until fully incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before transferring it to an air-tight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander creams helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's active ingredients, such as coriandrin and linalool, penetrate deep into the eye tissue, reducing swelling and redness. Additionally, coriander's natural antibacterial properties help to combat infections that can cause redness and discharge, promoting healthy eye function and clear vision. With regular use, coriander creams provide long-lasting relief from red eyes, leaving them feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Coriander contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and borneol, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in coriander, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to eye tissues, promoting healthy vision and reducing the appearance of red eyes.
Antibacterial properties: Coriander has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain pathogens that can contribute to eye infections and inflammation, which can lead to red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Coriander roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming red, irritated eyes.
Seeds: Coriander seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh coriander leaves and stems from organic sources and wash them thoroughly with cold water.
Blend the washed coriander with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a slow-speed blender until you get a smooth paste.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the paste and blend for an additional 30 seconds.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to melt the beeswax completely.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as citral and geraniol, help to reduce swelling and calm irritated tissues in the eyes. This relief from inflammation and irritation can quickly alleviate redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry or irritated eyes. Additionally, lemongrass' soothing and calming effects can also provide long-term benefits for overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can cause redness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemongrass cream reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause red, itchy, or watery eyes, promoting healthy eye tissue and reducing symptoms of conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and reduce redness in the eyes.
Rhizomes: Lemongrass rhizomes have natural antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial or fungal infections causing red eye issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them in shade for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried cymbopogon citratus into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10g of cymbopogon citratus powder with 20ml of coconut oil and heat gently over low flame.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid.
Mix the filtered liquid with 30g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter, then whip until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have potent antioxidant and soothing effects that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. This can provide relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with red eyes. By applying a herbal ginger cream to the affected area, you can calm the eyes and promote healing, leaving them feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can alleviate redness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage eye tissues and cause redness, puffiness, and fatigue, promoting a healthier and more comfortable eye environment.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's natural vasodilators, such as gingerol, help dilate blood vessels in the eyes, reducing congestion and inflammation that can contribute to redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help reduce puffiness and soothe redness in the eyes.
Buds: Containing natural antioxidants and flavonoids, buds help combat oxidative stress and promote healthy blood flow to the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried ginger root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground ginger powder with 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of beeswax and 1ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and continue heating until the wax melts for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 40°c, then stir in 10 drops of tea tree essential oil for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until ready to use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato creams helps with red eyes because they contain potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as chamomile and calendula, work to reduce puffiness and inflammation, while also providing a gentle cooling sensation that eases discomfort. As the cream is absorbed, it helps to restore the natural moisture balance of the eye area, leaving eyes feeling refreshed, cool, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild potato creams contain compounds like saponins and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in wild potato creams, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to redness and discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: Wild potato creams contain compounds like starches and mucilages that can help soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting faster healing of any minor abrasions or irritation that may be contributing to red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, roots help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum kok saghyz roots in the morning after dew has dried off.
Clean and dry the harvested roots for 24 hours before processing.
Grind 100g of cleaned roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 10 minutes.
Mix 30g of grated beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 50ml of distilled water in a double boiler for 1 hour.
Combine the powdered taraxacum kok saghyz roots with the beeswax mixture and stir until well combined then pour into sterilized jars within 15 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for red eyes are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate and treat red, itchy, and inflamed eyes. These creams are effective in soothing the skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The best herbal creams for red eyes include Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Euphrasia officinalis, Aloe vera, Hypericum perforatum, Echinacea purpurea, Euphorbia peplus, and Hamamelis virginiana. If left untreated, red eyes can lead to discomfort, fatigue, and decreased productivity, affecting daily routines, work, and social interactions.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal creams are various skin irritations and infections, such as acne, eczema, rosacea, and minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal creams containing soothing herbs like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile help calm inflammation and reduce discomfort, promoting healthy healing and reducing the risk of scarring. These natural remedies are often gentle enough for sensitive skin and can be used in conjunction with other treatments as directed by a healthcare professional.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with red eyes is a blend of Aloe vera, Peppermint, and Chamomile. Aloe vera soothes and calms the eyes, reducing inflammation. Peppermint helps to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of redness. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, further reduces swelling and irritation. This combination can be applied topically, providing quick relief from red, itchy, and tired eyes. Regular use can help maintain healthy, comfortable eyes.
